title: Analytic study and statistical enforcement of extended beta functions imposed by Mittag-Leffler and Hurwitz-Lerch Zeta functions
author: Abdulnabi, Al-Janaby, et al.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: In mathematics, complex analysis discusses the analytic functions of complex numbers. The term complex numbers acquire extensive utilization in various realms of mathematics, especially in functional analysis, see [[1], [2], [3], [4]]. The theory of special function (SFT) is a prominent and captivating theme in complex analysis. Special Functions (SFs) are created by solving numerous partial differential equations and physical problems, which are usually generalized formulas of elementary functions. At that point, SFs are mathematical functions that act in complex domains, have private merits, and are often named after the initials of scientists who innovated them. Notable examples include the hypergeometric function, Wright function, Mittag-Leffler function, Meijer G-function, Hurwitz zeta function, and others. Remarkably, diverse representations of SFs, such as power series (special series) or integral representations, are utilized in mathematical evaluation. This appeals in mathematical processes to create SFs, namely convolution (Hadamard) product and symbolized by *. It was first posed by Hadamard as [5]: for analytical power series Пү1(z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһПҒОә,1zОә and Пү2(z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһПҒОә,2zОә in consecutive disks О”1 and О”2 centered at the origin, the convolution product of Пү1 and Пү2, represented by Оё1*Оё2, produces a third power series expressed as:(1)(Пү1*Пү2)(z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһПҒОә,1ПҒОә,2zОә.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The vital idea of SFs lies in the effect of their complex parameters and variables in resolving and interpreting numerous scientific problems. Therefore, SFs are essential in developing ongoing investigations. Interestingly, Special Function Theory (SFT) is closely connected to Geometric Function Theory (GFT), which explores the geometric merits of analytical functions acting in a complex open unit disk. In this context, several investigators contributed greatly to its development, see ([6,7]). Indeed, the intensive study of SFs has occupied a major position in the scientific disciplines of applied mathematics; one can be referred to ([8,9]). The cornerstone of SFT is the Gamma function (Euler Integral of the second kind). Historically, the study of the Gamma function originally stemmed from a letter written by Euler to Goldbach in 1729, which is a more extended and generalized factorial function. It was rendered as [10]:(2)О“(Оҫ)=вҲ«0вҲһв„ҳОҫвҲ’1eвҲ’в„ҳdв„ҳ,(R(в„ҳ)>0).where is the analytic function in R(в„ҳ)>0. This function, as stated in (2), has attracted the interest of some researchers, such as Legendre and Gauss. In terms of (2), Pochhammer constructed a rising function, indicated by Pochhammer's symbol, as [10]:(3)(Оҫ)Оә=О“(Оҫ+Оә)О“(Оҫ)={Оҫ(Оҫ+1)вҖҰ(Оҫ+ОәвҲ’1),(ОәвҲҲN;ОҫвҲҲCвҲ–{0})1,(Оә=0).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: It plays a leading role in exploring many SFs, one of which is called Kummer functions. The class of Kummer function (confluent hypergeometric functions of the first kind) L(П„;Пұ;z) was first rendered by Kummer [11] in 1837 as:(4)1F1(П„;Пұ;z)=L(П„;Пұ;z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһ(П„)Оә(Пұ)ОәzОәОә!=О“(Пұ)О“(П„)вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһО“(П„+Оә)О“(Пұ+Оә)zОәОә!.where П„,ПұвҲҲC,Пұвү 0,вҲ’1,вҲ’2,вҖҰ and zвҲҲC. This function is a resolution to the following Kummer's differential equation(5)zd2в„ҳdz2+(П„вҲ’z)dв„ҳdzвҲ’П„dв„ҳ=0,

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The Kummer equation is a significant differential equation employed in various disciplines of engineering, chemistry, and physics [11]. The underlying cause for this significance is that many of the SFs of mathematical physics are expressed based on Kummer functions. The authors have continued the study of new classes of Kummer functions, along with their modifications, generalizations, and extensions, see [[12], [13], [14], [15]].

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: More generally, the following Gaussian (ordinary) hypergeometric function is posed as [16]:(6)2F1(П„,ПӮ;Пұ;z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһ(П„)Оә(ПӮ)Оә(Пұ)ОәzОәОә!.where П„,ПӮ,ПұвҲҲC,Пұвү 0,вҲ’1,вҲ’2,вҖҰ, and |z|<1, This function achieves the following second-order hypergeometric differential equation:(7)z(1вҲ’z)d2в„ҳdz2+[ПұвҲ’(П„+ПӮ+1)]dв„ҳdzвҲ’П„ПӮdв„ҳ=0.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The hypergeometric function is a generalized formula of the geometric series 2F1(1,ПӮ;ПӮ;z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһzОә. Further, the Kummer function given in (4) can be designated as a limit of the hypergeometric function: L(П„;Пұ;z)=limzвҶ’вҲһ2F1(П„,ПӮ;Пұ;ПӮвҲ’1z). Pursuing this thread of study, numerous researchers attempted to discover new classes of hypergeometric functions and have highlighted many new aspects of this area. In other words, the theory of hypergeometric functions is considered the most paramount and effective theme in several domains of mathematics. Specifically, the relevant role played by hypergeometric functions in GFT by resolving a major problem called "Bieberbach conjecture", is well known [17]. This theory was first studied by the infamous scholar Euler. The methodical study of hypergeometric functions began by Gauss in 1813, followed by the renowned scholars Kummer in 1836 and Riemann in 1857 (for details, see [16]). Since then, the hypergeometric functions have come under various formulas and have had several fruitful implementations. Works that have addressed this topic include those in ([18,19]).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: In this regard, corresponding to Gamma function (2), Mittag Leffler [20] in 1903 presented the function EОі(z) in the following formula:(8)EОі(z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһzОәО“(ОіОә+1),(R(Оі)>0,zвҲҲC).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: This one-parameter function is called a Mittag-Leffler function, which is a naturally generalized formula of exponential function ez. Then, Wiman ([21,22]) in 1905 proposed a more general EОі,Пұ(z) for (8) as:(9)EОі,Пұ(z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһzОәО“(ОіОә+Пұ),(R(Оі),R(Пұ)>0),which is named the two-parameter Mittag-Leffler function (Wiman function). Since then, the attention to Mittag-Leffler type functions has increased greatly, primarily due to their effort for applicability in diverse reaction-diffusion problems and the emergence of a variety of generalized formulas in resolutions of fractional (differential and integral) equations ([23,24]).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Analogous to Pochhammer's symbol (3), a novel interesting class of Mittag-Leffler-Kummer functions was considered by Ghanim et al. [12] in 2024 as:(10)ELП…,ПұПғ,Оі(z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһ(Оі)ПғОә(Пұ)П…ОәzОәОә!=О“(Пұ)О“(Оі)вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһО“(ПғОә+Оі)О“(П…Оә+Пұ)zОәОә!,(Оі,Пұ,Пғ,П…вҲҲC,Пұвү 0,вҲ’1,вҲ’2,вҖҰ,zвҲҲC),R(Оі),R(Пұ),R(Пғ),R(П…)>0.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Obviously, the Mittag-Leffler function and Kummer function are connected by their identities: ELОі,Пұ1,1(z)=EОі,Пұ(z); ELОі,11,1(z)=EОі(z); EL1,Пұ1,П„(z)=L(П„;Пұ;z).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Additionally, the effective aspect of SFs in the study of Analytic Number Theory (ANT) has given rise to the Hurwitz Lerch zeta (HL-Z) functions, indicated by HL(z,Ој,О·). This function is stated in [16,25] as:(11)HL(Ој,О·,z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһzОә(Оә+О·)Ој,where, ОјвҲҲC, О·вҲҲCвҲ–Z0вҲ’, when |z| 1, when |z|=1. This function continues meromorphically to C, (exclude for a simple pole at t=1 along with residue 1). Following that, Goyal and Laddha [26] in 1997 provided a more general HL-Z function:(12)HL*(П„,Ој,О·,z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһ(П„)ОәОә!zОә(Оә+О·)Ој,where, Ој,П„вҲҲC, О·вҲҲCвҲ–Z0вҲ’, when |z| 1, when |z|=1. Afterward, prominent authors offered a variety of appealing studies that involved HL-Z functions ([27,28]).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: In this regard, one of the great functions in SFT is the Beta function, also termed Euler's Integral of the first kind, which has major roles in developing scientific research, such as engineering and mathematics. It is formulated as [10]:(13)B(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=вҲ«01в„ҳОҫ1вҲ’1(1вҲ’в„ҳ)Оҫ2вҲ’1dв„ҳ,(R(Оҫ1),R(Оҫ2)>0).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Besides, closely connected to the Beta function is the Gamma function given by (2) as [10]:(14)B(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=О“(Оҫ1+Оҫ2)О“(Оҫ1)О“(Оҫ2).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: In the 1990s, the study of extended Gamma and Beta functions became a catalyst theme of scientific research that attracted investigators. The extended Gamma function (2) was first presented by Chaudhry and Zubair [29] in 1994 as follows:(15)О“Оҙ(Оҫ)=вҲ«0вҲһв„ҳОҫвҲ’1eвҲ’в„ҳвҲ’Оҙв„ҳdв„ҳ,(R(в„ҳ),Оҙ>0).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Importantly, the role played by special classes of analytic functions, especially exponential, Kummer, and Mittag-Leffler functions, as kernels is of great significance for rendering new versions of extended Beta functions. In 1997, Chaudhry et al. [30] postulated the first extended Beta function that includes the exponential function as kernel, as follows:(16)BОҙ(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=вҲ«01в„ҳОҫ1вҲ’1(1вҲ’в„ҳ)Оҫ2вҲ’1eвҲ’Оҙв„ҳ(1вҲ’в„ҳ)dв„ҳ,(R(Оҫ1),R(Оҫ2),R(Оҙ)>0).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: For Оҙ=0 in (16), it turns to B(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) given in (13). Later, in 2011, Г–zergin et al. [31] suggested and analyzed the more general extended Beta function based on (4) as a kernel. It is instituted as:(17)BОҙ,ПұП„(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=вҲ«01в„ҳОҫ1вҲ’1(1вҲ’в„ҳ)Оҫ2вҲ’11F1(П„;Пұ;вҲ’Оҙв„ҳ(1вҲ’в„ҳ))dв„ҳ,(min{R(Оҫ1),R(Оҫ2),R(Оі),R(Пұ),R(Оҙ)}>0).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Obviously, for П„=Пұ in (17), it yields to BОҙ(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) given by (16). If Оҙ=0 and П„=Пұ in (17), it reduces to B(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) written by (13).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: In 2017, Pucheta [32] discussed the different extended Beta functions in terms of (8) as a kernel given as:(18)BОҙОі(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=вҲ«0вҲһв„ҳОҫ1вҲ’1(1вҲ’в„ҳ)Оҫ2вҲ’1EОі(вҲ’Оҙв„ҳ(1вҲ’в„ҳ))dв„ҳ,(min{R(Оҫ1),R(Оҫ1),R(Оі),R(Оҙ)}>0).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Notice that for Оҙ=0 and Оі=1 in (18), it gains to B(в„ҳ1,в„ҳ2) defined by (13). Analogous to Pucheta, Shadab et al. [33] in 2018 presented another extended Beta function by employing (8) as a kernel. It is stated as follows:(19)BОҙОі(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=вҲ«01в„ҳОҫ1вҲ’1(1вҲ’в„ҳ)Оҫ2вҲ’1EОі(вҲ’Оҙв„ҳвҲ’1(1вҲ’в„ҳ)вҲ’1)dв„ҳ,(min{R(Оҫ1),R(Оҫ1),R(Оі),R(Оҙ)}>0).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: For Оі=1 in (19), it achieves to BОҙ(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) given by (16). If Оҙ=0 and Оі=1 in (19), it acquires to B(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) expressed by (13).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: In 2021, Goyal et al. [34] investigated the extended Beta function by utilizing (9) as a kernel. It is coined as:(20)BОҙ(Оі;Пұ)(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=вҲ«01в„ҳОҫ1вҲ’1(1вҲ’в„ҳ)Оҫ2вҲ’1EОі,Пұ(вҲ’Оҙ(в„ҳ(1вҲ’в„ҳ))вҲ’1)dв„ҳ,(min{R(Оҫ1),R(Оҫ2),R(Оі),R(Пұ),R(Оҙ)}>0).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Evidently, for Пұ=1 in (20), it turns to BОҙОі(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) written by (19). If Оі=Пұ=1 in (20), it reduces to BОҙ(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) given by (16). Also, if Оҙ=0 and Оі=Пұ=1 in (20), it arrives to B(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) posed in (13).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: In 2024, Ghanim et al. [35] presented a new extended Beta function called Euler's Beta Mittag-Leffler-Kummer function by employing (10) as a kernel. It is formulated as:(21)BОҙ,П…,Пұ(Пғ,Оі)(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=вҲ«01в„ҳОҫ1вҲ’1(1вҲ’в„ҳ)Оҫ2вҲ’1ELП…,ПұПғ,Оі(вҲ’Оҙ(в„ҳ(1вҲ’в„ҳ))вҲ’1)dв„ҳ,(min{R(Оҫ1),R(Оҫ2),R(Оі),R(Пұ),R(Оҙ)}>0).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: For Пғ=Оі=1 in (21), it turns to О“(Пұ)BОҙ(Оі;Пұ)(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) given in (15). If Пғ=Оі=Пұ=1 in (21), it reduces to BОҙОі(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) posed in (19). If Пғ=Оі=Пұ=П…=1 in (21), it yields to BОҙ(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) written in (16). Further, if Пғ=Оі=Пұ=П…=1 and Оҙ=0 in (21), it reduces to B(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) given in (13).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: In mathematics, the realm of Fractional Calculus (FC) has played a remarkable role and is considered a dynamic tool for modeling and solving manifold problems in engineering, combined with pure and applied areas of sciences, see [[36], [37], [38]]. It was systematically developed in the 19th century. This realm deals with arbitrary (real or complex) order of integration and differentiation operations and is the generalized formula of classical calculus. The idea of FC was first put forward by legendary scientists in a letter from L'Hopital addressed to Leibniz in 1695, in which the question of the sense of the semi-derivative was raised. Subsequently, several great mathematicians, such as Euler, Riemann, Liouville, Fourier, Laplace, and others, contributed significantly to the evolution of FC. In this line, SFT is considered the cornerstone of the theoretical formulation of FC, which led to its emergence and evolution. In other words, the theoretical construction of fractional (integral and derivative) operators is often characterized in terms of the Gamma function written in (2). Prominent examples of this include the traditional Riemann-Liouville (RL) fractional derivative of Пү(z) of order q formulated as [39]:DzqПү(z)=1О“(вҲ’q)вҲ«0z(zвҲ’в„ҳ)вҲ’qвҲ’1Пү(в„ҳ)dв„ҳ,where R(q)<0. For R(q)вүҘ0, consider lвҲҲN such that lвҲ’1вүӨR(q)<l. Then, RL fractional derivative of Пү(z) of order q is expressed as:DzqПү(z)=dldzlDzqвҲ’lПү(z)=dldzl{1О“(lвҲ’q)вҲ«0z(zвҲ’в„ҳ)lвҲ’qвҲ’1Пү(в„ҳ)dв„ҳ}.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Then, obtaining formulas of RL-type fractional derivatives correlated with a variety of SFs of interest is currently investigated; see ([40,41]). By pursuing the interesting previous studies in developing the theme of extended Beta functions, this sequel continues to present an analysis by using the eminent SFs called generalization of Mittag-Leffler and Hurwitz Lerch zeta functions as kernel to render new extended Beta functions. This study introduces a new generalized Beta function in terms of the Mittag-Leffler function (9) and HL-Z function (12) utilizing a convolution structure. Moreover, diverse analytical merits of this posed Beta function are provided, including functional and summation relations, Mellin transforms, integral representations, and assorted derivative formulas. Furthermore, the statistical implementation of the extended Beta distribution is also achieved.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: In this paper, our approach for constructing extended Beta functions explicitly integrates Mittag-Leffler and Hurwitz-Lerch Zeta (HLZ) functions as kernels, using the convolution technique to get an extended Beta function. This dual-function structure improves analytical depth and flexibility, enabling multi-parameter generalizations and broader applicability. This research advances the statistical implementation by applying the suggested Beta function to the enlarged Beta distribution. Accordingly, it gives a comprehensive framework for statistical modeling, improving applications in probability theory and statistical science. Our methodology presents novel extended ordinary hypergeometric functions and extended Kummer functions, which are derived from the suggested Beta function. These contributions address a deficiency in the current literature by extending these functions within a cohesive and generalized approach. This effort improves upon previous endeavors by obtaining fractional derivatives of the expanded Beta function. Integral representations and generation functions are introduced to improve the theoretical context of these functions. The resources that these improvements provide are substantial and can be used to address higher-order differential equations and series expansions. For more details see [39,42] and [43].

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Proposed Beta- Mittag-Leffler-Hurwitz-Lerch Zeta Function BОҙ,Пұ,О·П„,Ој(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: This section presents a new extended Beta function, indicated by BОҙ,Пұ,О·П„,Ој(Оҫ1,Оҫ2), related to the Mittag-Leffler (9) and the HL-Z (12) functions using the convolution principle (1).

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: The Mittag-Leffler function (Wiman's function) is expressed in (9) as:EОі,Пұ(z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһzОәО“(ОәОі+Пұ),(R(Оі),R(Пұ)>0).

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: For Оі=1, the function EОі,Пұ(z) turns to(22)E1,Пұ(z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһzОәО“(Оә+Пұ),(R(Пұ)>0).

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Furthermore, the HL-Z function HL*(z,П„,Ој,О·) is stated in (12) as:HL*(,П„,Ој,О·,z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһ(П„)ОәОә!zОә(Оә+О·)Ој,(Ој,П„вҲҲC, О·вҲҲCвҲ–Z0вҲ’, when |z| 1, when |z|=1).

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Thus, from (1), (22), and (12), the following new function is acquired:(23)ПҲПұ,О·П„,Ој(z)=HL*(П„,Ој,О·,z)*О“(Пұ)E1,Пұ(z)=вҲ‘Оә=0вҲһ(П„)Оә(Пұ)Оә(Оә+О·)ОјzОәОә!.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Remark 1. For the applicable parameters, П„,Ој and Пұ, the function ПҲПұ,О·П„,Ој(z) written in (23) reduces to specified functions. Hence, the following special cases are yielded:1.ПҲ1,О·1,0(z)=ez,2.ПҲПұ,О·П„,0(z)=1F1(П„;Пұ;z) given in (4).

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Based on Eq. (23), the new extended Beta function BОҙ,Пұ,О·П„,Ој(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) is expressed by the following definition.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Definition 1. For ОҙвүҘ0, the extended Beta function BОҙ,Пұ,О·П„,Ој(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) is formulated as:(24)BОҙ,Пұ,О·П„,Ој(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=вҲ«01в„ҳОҫ1вҲ’1(1вҲ’в„ҳ)Оҫ2вҲ’1ПҲПұ,О·П„,Ој(вҲ’Оҙ(в„ҳ(1вҲ’в„ҳ))вҲ’1)dв„ҳ,where min{R(Оҫ1),R(Оҫ2),R(П„),R(Ој),R(Пұ),R(О·)}>0.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Remark 2. The noteworthy special cases of extended Beta function BОҙ,Пұ,О·П„,Ој(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) are:1.BОҙ,Пұ,О·П„,0(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=BОҙ,ПұП„(Оҫ1,Оҫ2) stated in (17),2.BОҙ,1,О·1,0(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=BОҙ(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)given by (16),3.B0,1,О·1,0(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)=B(Оҫ1,Оҫ2)written by (13).
